# Store Channel Policy

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/storecontract -write-policy-table. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to Store Channel Policy](../store-channel-policy.md)

Executable SSOT:

- [`policy-table.riido.json`](policy-table.riido.json)
- [`riido_daemon_store_distribution.riido.json`](../../../../packaging/store/riido_daemon_store_distribution.riido.json)
- `internal/policy.EvaluateStoreChannelPolicy`

| Surface | `developer-id` | `mac-app-store` | `msix-sideload` | `msix-store` |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Provider CLI bundling | forbidden | forbidden | forbidden | forbidden |
| Provider CLI user-selected path | allowed | requires os-grant + store-review | allowed | allowed |
| Silent provider auto-install | forbidden | forbidden | forbidden | forbidden |
| Background helper | requires explicit-consent | requires explicit-consent + store-review | requires explicit-consent | requires explicit-consent + store-review |
| Direct LaunchAgent install | requires explicit-consent | forbidden | not applicable | not applicable |
| Windows service install | not applicable | not applicable | discouraged | forbidden |
| External TCP listener | forbidden | forbidden | forbidden | forbidden |
| Local IPC | allowed | allowed | allowed | allowed |
| Self-updater | allowed | forbidden | allowed | forbidden |
| Arbitrary home scan | forbidden | forbidden | forbidden | forbidden |

## Evaluation Rules

- The table is generated by executing the C7 store channel policy decision function.
- `requires ...` means the surface is allowed only when those reduced facts are true.
- The tool does not inspect entitlements, install helpers, open IPC, or execute provider CLIs.
- `dev-local` is not a package channel; policy code treats it as the Developer ID relaxation axis.
